conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Content Constraints
seed_instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting.
org_constraint: The email must include specific reasons for my resignation, such as career growth opportunities or personal circumstances.
new_constraint: The phrase "I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning" must appear at least once in the email.
instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. The email must include specific reasons for my resignation, such as career growth opportunities or personal circumstances. The phrase "I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning" must appear at least once in the email.
response:
Subject: Resignation - [Your Name]

Dear [Boss's Name],

Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Your Job Title] at [Company Name]. My last day will be [Your Last Date].

This was a difficult decision to make, but I have decided to move on to a new chapter in my life. While I have truly valued my time here and appreciate the opportunities I've had to work with such a talented team, I have realized that my current role no longer aligns with my long-term career growth objectives. I am seeking a position that offers a different trajectory for my professional development, and I believe it is the right time for me to pursue that path elsewhere.

Regarding the specific details behind this decision, I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning at this time. I hope you understand that this choice is based on a combination of personal circumstances and my evolving career goals, rather than any dissatisfaction with the company or my colleagues.

Over the next [Number] weeks, I am fully committed to ensuring a smooth transition. Please let me know how I can best assist with handing over my current projects, documenting my workflows, or training my replacement.

Thank you for the support and guidance you have provided me during my tenure. I wish you and the company all the best for the future.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Job Title]
